openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4.6.output: {"A":"Answer A is accurate and follows all instructions. It implements a random joke generator with 4 jokes and a button. It goes well beyond the requirements with Tailwind CSS styling, confetti animations, 3D tilt effects, joke counters, and setup/punchline formatting. While impressive, it includes significant unnecessary complexity (confetti system, external CDN dependency, blob animations, 3D mouse tracking) that wasn't requested. The code is functional but harder to understand due to its complexity.","B":"Answer B is accurate and follows all instructions precisely. It implements exactly what was asked: a button that displays one of four random jokes. The code is clean, self-contained with no external dependencies, well-commented, and easy to understand. It includes helpful instructions for how to use the file and a clear explanation of how it works. The styling is appropriate without being excessive.","winner":"B","reason":"While Answer A demonstrates impressive technical skill with many extra features, Answer B better serves the prompt by providing a simple, clean, self-contained solution that directly addresses what was asked. Answer B is more concise, easier to understand, requires no external dependencies, and includes helpful documentation. The prompt asked for a 'simple website' and Answer B delivers exactly that, whereas Answer A adds significant unnecessary complexity. For a beginner or someone who just needs the functionality described, Answer B is clearly more appropriate and useful."}

messages.content: Create a simple website using HTML. When the user clicks a button, one of four jokes should be displayed at random.
